name: Identity & Culture
description: Talking about yourself, relationships, customs and traditions in French-speaking countries

subskills.name: Relationships & What Makes You Who You Are

commonMisconceptions: Confusing 'je suis' (I am) with 'j'ai' (I have) when describing age - French uses 'j'ai 15 ans' not 'je suis 15 ans'
Forgetting that adjectives must agree in gender and number with the noun they describe
Using 'c'est' when 'il est/elle est' is required before an adjective without a noun
ocrRef: OCR J730 Topic 1: Home and abroad

name: School & Education
description: School life, subjects, opinions on education and school rules
commonMisconceptions: 'College' in French means secondary school (ages 11-15), not university
Confusing 'cours' (lesson) with 'cour' (playground/courtyard)
Thinking French schools have the same structure as British ones - they do not have sixth form in the same way
aqaRef: AQA 8652 Theme 1: People and Lifestyle - School
edexcelRef: Edexcel 1FR0 Theme 3: Current and future study and employment
ocrRef: OCR J730 Topic 2: Education and employment

name: Free Time
description: Hobbies, sports, music, film and leisure activities
commonMisconceptions: 'Jouer a' is for sports and games, 'jouer de' is for musical instruments - mixing these up is very common
'Faire de la natation' not 'jouer a la natation' - some sports use 'faire' rather than 'jouer'
Forgetting to use the correct article after 'jouer a' and 'jouer de' (au, a la, aux, du, de la, des)

name: Holidays & Travel
description: Holiday experiences, booking accommodation, travel arrangements and transport
commonMisconceptions: Forgetting that 'aller' uses etre as its auxiliary in the passe compose, not avoir
Confusing the imperfect tense (descriptions/ongoing) with the passe compose (completed actions) when writing about holidays
Not knowing that 'vacances' is always plural in French - 'les vacances'
aqaRef: AQA 8652 Theme 3: Communication and the World Around Us
edexcelRef: Edexcel 1FR0 Theme 2: Local, national, international and global areas of interest
ocrRef: OCR J730 Topic 1: Home and abroad

name: Family & Friends
description: Family members, describing relationships, friendship and marriage
commonMisconceptions: 'Mon amie' vs 'ma copine' - 'copain/copine' can mean boyfriend/girlfriend, not just friend
Possessive adjectives agree with the noun, not the owner: 'sa mere' means his/her mother
Reflexive verbs like 's'entendre' need the reflexive pronoun to change with the subject
aqaRef: AQA 8652 Theme 1: People and Lifestyle - Relationships
ocrRef: OCR J730 Topic 1: Home and abroad

name: Town & Local Area
description: Describing where you live, giving directions, shops and local facilities
commonMisconceptions: 'Bibliotheque' means library, not bookshop - 'librairie' means bookshop (a classic false friend)
Prepositions with places: 'a' for towns, 'en' for feminine countries, 'au' for masculine countries
'Il y a' is invariable - it does not change regardless of singular or plural nouns following it
aqaRef: AQA 8652 Theme 3: Communication and the World Around Us - Local area
ocrRef: OCR J730 Topic 1: Home and abroad

name: Social Issues
description: Homelessness, poverty, charity work, equality and volunteering
commonMisconceptions: 'SDF' stands for 'sans domicile fixe' - students often do not recognise this abbreviation in reading texts
Confusing 'sain' (healthy) with 'saint' (holy/saint)
Using informal language for a topic that typically requires formal register in writing tasks
aqaRef: AQA 8652 Theme 3: Communication and the World Around Us - Social issues
ocrRef: OCR J730 Topic 3: Current and future study and employment

name: Environment
description: Environmental problems, sustainability, recycling and protecting the planet

subskills.name: Environmental Problems
keywords: les gaz a effet de serre

commonMisconceptions: Using 'il faut' without the infinitive - it must be followed by an infinitive verb
Confusing the conditional 'on devrait' with the present 'on doit' when making suggestions vs obligations
'Sauver' means to save (rescue), while 'economiser' means to save (money/resources)
aqaRef: AQA 8652 Theme 3: Communication and the World Around Us - Environment
ocrRef: OCR J730 Topic 4: Environment and social issues

name: Technology
description: Social media, mobile phones, internet use, advantages and disadvantages of technology
commonMisconceptions: 'Portable' in French usually means mobile phone, not a laptop (which is 'un ordinateur portable')
Forgetting to use connectives that show two sides of an argument for higher marks
'Actuellement' means 'currently' not 'actually' - a very common false friend
ocrRef: OCR J730 Topic 3: Media and technology

name: Future Plans
description: Career aspirations, further education, work experience and ambitions
commonMisconceptions: Many job titles have masculine and feminine forms in French - students often only learn one form
Confusing 'un stage' (work placement) with 'stage' in English (a platform)
Using only 'je veux' (I want) instead of the more polite 'je voudrais' (I would like) in formal contexts
aqaRef: AQA 8652 Theme 1: People and Lifestyle - Future plans
edexcelRef: Edexcel 1FR0 Theme 3: Current and future study and employment
ocrRef: OCR J730 Topic 2: Education and employment

name: Grammar Essentials
description: Core grammar structures needed across all topics: tenses, agreements, negatives and pronouns
commonMisconceptions: Not all past tense uses passe compose - the imperfect is needed for descriptions, habitual actions and ongoing states
Avoir verbs in passe compose do NOT agree with the subject, but etre verbs DO
The conditional is not just 'would' - it is essential for polite requests and hypothetical situations
Negatives wrap around the verb: 'je ne mange pas' - both parts are required
ocrRef: OCR J730 Grammar across all topics

name: Translation Skills
description: Techniques for translating between English and French accurately in both directions
commonMisconceptions: 'Assister a' means to attend, not to assist - 'aider' means to assist
'Rester' means to stay, not to rest - 'se reposer' means to rest
Literal word-for-word translation rarely works - French word order and structure differ from English
'La journee' emphasises the duration of the day, while 'le jour' is the day as a unit of time
